title: viola canina subsp. lusitana
intro: Viola canina subsp. lusitana, also known as the Wood Pansy or Wild Pansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Viola canina subsp. lusitana include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stress. Herbal preparations of Viola canina subsp. lusitana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to promote overall well-being.
